heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 27 May 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 1 heatwaves; typically, 1.8 heatwaves happen every year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (227°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.8°C) and outside temperature (down 1.9°C) and drier conditions in outside humidity (down 5%).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 5.5°C), feels like temperature (up 7.7°C), outside temperature (up 4.9°C), and inside temperature (up 1.9°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 663.9m higher than yesterday, 319.4m higher than the past week, 1135.4m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1381m minimum and 5470m maximum, with an average of 3740m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 3420m and 2600m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will remain comfortably hot. Outside; hot temperatures will prevail. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and winds will be practically absent. Possible light showers, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
